source_excerpt: "Is she injured?" I asked of Sola, indicating Dejah Thoris by an inclination of my head.

speaker.notes: First-person narration suggests John Carter as the speaker.

source_excerpt: "No," she answered, "she thinks that you are dead."

speaker.notes: Context suggests a female speaker, but the source wording is still ambiguous.

dialogue_text: she thinks that you are dead.
source_excerpt: "No," she answered, "she thinks that you are dead."

speaker.notes: Context suggests a female speaker, but the source wording is still ambiguous.

dialogue_text: And that her grandmother's cat may now have no one to polish its teeth?
source_excerpt: "And that her grandmother's cat may now have no one to polish its teeth?" I queried, smiling.

speaker.notes: First-person narration suggests John Carter as the speaker.

dialogue_text: I think you wrong her, John Carter,
source_excerpt: "I think you wrong her, John Carter," said Sola. "I do not understand either her ways or yours, but I am sure the granddaughter of ten thousand jeddaks would never grieve like this over any who held but the highest claim upon her affecti...

dialogue_text: Tears are a strange sight upon Barsoom,
source_excerpt: "Tears are a strange sight upon Barsoom," she continued, "and so it is difficult for me to interpret them. I have seen but two people weep in all my life, other than Dejah Thoris; one wept from sorrow, the other from baffled rage. The fi...

speaker.notes: First-person narration suggests John Carter as the speaker.

dialogue_text: and so it is difficult for me to interpret them. I have seen but two people weep in all my life, other than Dejah Thoris; one wept from sorrow, the other from baffled rage. The first was my mother, years ago before they killed her; the other was Sarkoja, when they dragged her from me today.

source_excerpt: "Your mother!" I exclaimed, "but, Sola, you could not have known your mother, child."

speaker.notes: First-person narration suggests John Carter as the speaker.

dialogue_text: But I did. And my father also,
source_excerpt: "But I did. And my father also," she added. "If you would like to hear the strange and un-Barsoomian story come to the chariot tonight, John Carter, and I will tell you that of which I have never spoken in all my life before. And now the...

speaker.notes: First-person narration suggests John Carter as the speaker.

dialogue_text: If you would like to hear the strange and un-Barsoomian story come to the chariot tonight, John Carter, and I will tell you that of which I have never spoken in all my life before. And now the signal has been given to resume the march, you must go.
source_excerpt: "But I did. And my father also," she added. "If you would like to hear the strange and un-Barsoomian story come to the chariot tonight, John Carter, and I will tell you that of which I have never spoken in all my life before. And now the...

speaker.notes: First-person narration suggests John Carter as the speaker.

source_excerpt: "I will come tonight, Sola," I promised. "Be sure to tell Dejah Thoris I am alive and well. I shall not force myself upon her, and be sure that you do not let her know I saw her tears. If she would speak with me I but await her command."

speaker.notes: First-person narration suggests John Carter as the speaker.
